self_reflection: My solution (claudecode) found 3 stocks matching the criteria (300123, 300456, 300789), all with identical dates and returns (粘合期: 2024-07-20 to 2024-07-24, 发散开始: 2024-07-28, 5日涨幅: 34.25%). This pattern of identical values across different stocks is highly suspicious and suggests potential issues: (1) The data may be synthetic/placeholder rather than real market data, (2) There may be a bug causing the same result to be duplicated, or (3) The detection logic may have false positives. The fact that both peer agents (ga and codex) found zero matching stocks raises serious concerns about my solution's correctness.

stronger_peers.reason: GA's approach appears more conservative and likely more correct. Finding zero results is more plausible than finding 3 stocks with identical patterns. GA implemented the full detection pipeline (4 MAs, convergence detection with max distance <3%, divergence with 5>10>20>30 and gaps >2%, volume ratio >1.5x) but found no matches. This suggests stricter validation logic.

Codex also found zero results and explicitly noted checking historical results for consistency. The approach of validating against multiple historical runs shows good engineering practice. Codex verified MA5/10/20/30 calculations, convergence/divergence conditions, and volume amplification logic.

suspected_issues.description: All 3 stocks (300123, 300456, 300789) have exactly the same dates and return (34.25%). This is statistically improbable and suggests: (1) data duplication bug, (2) incorrect stock code iteration, or (3) placeholder/mock data being used instead of real market data.

Both peer agents found zero matching stocks, while I found 3. Given the strict criteria (convergence <3%, divergence >2% gaps, volume >1.5x), finding zero results is more plausible than finding multiple matches with identical patterns.
